title: Using Present to Talk About the Past
description: Learn to use 'haber' to form the present perfect tense in Spanish.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: We can use the present in Spanish to talk about the past, just as in English. For example: I ate, I was eating, and I have eaten. We'll focus on 'I have eaten', which uses 'haber'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: The verb 'haber' is used for 'have' in 'I have eaten'. It's irregular, but we can remember its forms by focusing on its vowel sounds, /a/ and /e/. What are these vowel sounds?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Let's start with 'he', which means 'I have'. It's spelled h-e. Now, what was the verb for 'to know'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Saber. And 'I know' is 'sé'. Just like 'sé', 'he' is the I form of 'haber'. What do you think 'we have' is?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hemos. Good. From 'haber', we get 'he' and 'hemos'. Now, 'ha' means 'he/she/it has'. What about 'they have'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Han. Good. And 'you have' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Has. Great. So, we have 'he-hemos', 'ha-has-han'. Now, to form the past participle for -ar verbs, remove -ar and add -ado. For example, 'hablar' becomes:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablado. Now, let's say 'I have spoken':

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: He hablado. Good. How about 'I haven't spoken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No he hablado. Good. 'We have spoken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hemos hablado. 'He has spoken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Ha hablado. 'They have spoken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Han hablado. 'You (informal) have spoken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Has hablado. Perfect. Now, let's use 'anticipar'. How would you say 'we have anticipated it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Lo hemos anticipado. Good. How about 'we have anticipated it for a long time'?

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: lo hemos anticipado por mucho tiempo

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Lo hemos anticipado por mucho tiempo. Very good. Now, let's ask 'have you celebrated?' informally.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Has celebrado? Good. 'Haven't you celebrated?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No has celebrado. 'Where have you celebrated it?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Dónde lo has celebrado. Good. Now, let's use 'tomar'. How would you say 'I have taken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: He tomado. 'We have taken'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hemos tomado. 'Has she taken it?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Lo ha tomado. Good. Remember to think about each part of the sentence separately to build it correctly.

outro.text: You've learned how to use 'haber' to form the present perfect tense in Spanish.
Try forming sentences with other verbs, like 'comer' or 'vivir', using the present perfect tense.
This tense is very common in Spanish, so practice using it in different contexts.
